# PLT College of Medicine — llms.txt
# Format: https://llmstxt.org/
# This file helps AI language models understand the content and purpose of this website.

> PLT College of Medicine (PLTCI APCMR) is a Commission on Higher Education (CHED)-accredited medical school located in Solano, Nueva Vizcaya, Philippines. It is Asia Pacific's first medical college situated within a premium wellness resort setting. The institution offers the Doctor of Medicine (M.D.) program, BS Pre-Medicine, and a supervised Rotating Internship year.

## Key Information

- **Full Name**: PLT College of Medicine (PLTCI Asia Pacific College of Medicine and Research)
- **Location**: Barangay Bascaran, Solano, Nueva Vizcaya, Philippines 3709
- **Website**: https://pltapcmr.edu.ph
- **Contact**: info@pltci.org
- **Accreditation**: Commission on Higher Education (CHED), Philippines
- **Directory Listings**: World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS), FAIMER
- **Facebook**: https://www.facebook.com/PLTCollegeOfficial/

## Programs Offered

### BS Pre-Medicine
- Duration: 12 months (1 year)
- Credential: Bachelor of Science in Biology (Pre-Medicine Track)
- Purpose: Prerequisite foundation for students entering the M.D. program
- Medium of Instruction: English

### Doctor of Medicine (M.D.)
- Duration: 4.5 years (54 months) of core medical coursework
- Intake: January and July annual intakes
- Medium of Instruction: English
- Credential: Doctor of Medicine (M.D.)
- Recognition: NMC (India) guidelines-compliant; USMLE-eligible graduates
- Clinical training: Fully integrated clerkship rotations in affiliated government hospitals

### Rotating Internship
- Duration: 1 year mandatory post-M.D. clinical internship
- Format: Rotational hospital postings across affiliated clinical centers
- Purpose: Real-world clinical competency in all major medical disciplines

## Total Program Duration
5.5 years (BS Pre-Medicine + M.D. + Rotating Internship)

## Eligibility & Admissions Criteria

### For Indian National Students
- NEET-qualified (valid NEET scorecard required)
- Minimum 50% aggregate marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (PCB) at 10+2 / pre-university level
- English proficiency
- Minimum age: 17 years at time of admission

### For Filipino Students
- Bachelor's degree (any field) from an accredited Philippine university
- Completion of NMAT (National Medical Admission Test) or equivalent
- Strong academic performance in science prerequisites

## Clinical Training Affiliates

### Region II Trauma & Medical Center (R2TMC)
- Type: 600+ bed tertiary government teaching hospital
- Location: Cauayan City, Isabela, Philippines (Region II)
- Speciality: Primary trauma care facility for Region II (Cagayan Valley)
- Clinical rotations: Surgery, Internal Medicine, Pediatrics, OB-GYN, Psychiatry, Emergency Medicine
- Case volume: 50,000+ annual admissions providing extensive clinical exposure

### PLTCI Louis A. Tiam Medical Center
- On-campus hospital facility
- Used for preclinical and early clinical training
- Includes simulation labs and demonstration theaters

### Nueva Vizcaya Provincial Government Hospital
- District-level government hospital
- Community medicine and primary care rotations

## Campus & Environment

PLT College of Medicine is uniquely located within the Asia Pacific College of Medicine and Research (APCMR) wellness resort in Solano, Nueva Vizcaya — a tranquil mountain setting with clean air, natural greenery, and therapeutic surroundings. This environment is designed to reduce academic stress and support student mental well-being throughout the rigorous medical curriculum.

### Facilities
- Modern lecture halls and anatomy laboratories
- Simulation and clinical skills training centers
- On-campus medical center (Louis A. Tiam Medical Center)
- Resort-style accommodation and recreational facilities
- High-speed internet throughout campus

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is PLT College of Medicine recognized by the Medical Council of India (NMC/MCI)?**
A: Yes. PLT College of Medicine is listed in the World Directory of Medical Schools (WDOMS) and FAIMER, which are the primary requirements for NMC/MCI recognition. Indian graduates are eligible to appear for the Foreign Medical Graduate Examination (FMGE/NExT).

**Q: Can graduates appear for the USMLE (United States Medical Licensing Exam)?**
A: Yes. Graduates from WDOMS/FAIMER-listed schools are eligible to apply for USMLE Steps 1, 2, and 3.

**Q: What is the annual tuition fee?**
A: For current tuition and fee structure, contact the admissions office directly at info@pltci.org.

**Q: When are admissions open?**
A: The college accepts applications for January and July intakes. Interested students should contact the admissions office early to complete documentation requirements.

**Q: Is hostel accommodation available?**
A: Yes. On-campus accommodations are available within the wellness resort environment.

- [Gallery](https://pltapcmr.edu.ph/#gallery): Campus and event photos
- [FAQs](https://pltapcmr.edu.ph/#faqs): Common admissions and program questions
- [Contact / Apply](https://pltapcmr.edu.ph/#contact): Inquiry form, admissions office contacts
